trade.summary: Limited bilateral trade. Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, and rubber; Poland exports machinery, chemicals, and food products. Trade is largely conducted via broader EU-ASEAN channels. Intel and other US chip companies with Malaysian operations supply Polish electronics assemblers.

diplomatic.summary: Normal but limited diplomatic engagement. Both countries cooperate through multilateral frameworks. Poland's EU membership mediates much of the relationship. No significant bilateral disputes or special partnerships.

military.summary: No bilateral military relationship. Poland is a NATO member with growing defense orientation; Malaysia is non-aligned within ASEAN.

financial.summary: Very limited direct investment. Some Polish logistics companies operate in Malaysian supply chains. Malaysian palm oil investors have limited EU exposure via Poland.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Poland relies primarily on coal and gas from domestic and EU-proximate sources.

scientific.summary: Minimal scientific cooperation. Some student exchange programs. Joint research through EU-funded multilateral frameworks.
